{{Quote|Nobody, not even the Zanibar cannibals of Xo, wants to get on the Guild's bad side.|[[Arack Dostryt|Arack "Deadeye" Dostryt]]|<ref name="The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>}}
The '''Zanibar''' were a [[Cannibalism|cannibalistic]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Xo]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Zanibar meat is delicious|[[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]] on the Zanibar|<ref name="The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett" />}}
The Zanibar were tall and thin [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with [[Color/Legends|grayish-blue]] skin and three-fingered hands. They were bald and had long skull-like faces with small black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="CEII3" />

The [[Species/Legends|species]] occupied at least one village on its [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Xo, where the Zanibar performed a sacrificial ceremony believed to involve a [[Cannibalism|feast]] of their victims' bodies to give their life forces to the Zanibar [[Deity/Legends|gods]].<ref name="Handbook" />

==History==
Prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Zanibar cannibals were involved in an incident in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] which created a lasting enmity between rival businessmen [[Dominic Raynor]] and [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark]].<ref name="Wheel">{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

Some Zanibar warriors became [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] or [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]], accepting only sentients as payment that they could bring back to Xo and sacrifice, including a group allied with the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Grappa (Hutt)|Grappa]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] under the leadership of [[Zzzanmxl]]<ref name="CEII3" /> in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]].<ref name="Date">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] dates the events of ''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' to 11 ABY.</ref> These Zanibar were unwilling to work with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], instead relying on Grappa's services as a go-between.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|367|Zanibar}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Zanibar first appeared in the [[November]] [[1998]] [[Dark Horse Comics]] issue [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 1|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 1]], written by [[Mike Richardson]] and [[Randy Stradley]] and illustrated by [[Paul Gulacy]].<ref name="CEII1">[[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 1|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 1]]</ref>
